key_insights.insight: Early struggles and being an outsider can forge a unique creative identity.
key_insights.quote: I wouldn't have had such a definitive like who I am

key_insights.insight: Discipline, even when initially forced, can instill a valuable work ethic that separates you from others.
key_insights.quote: He gave me the most complex rules of what discipline means, what it is to apply in whatever you're doing. And I didn't realize that until I was older.

key_insights.insight: Passion and a love for music are more important than technical skill in creating a successful career.
key_insights.quote: They didn't really care about the outcome. They cared about like the passion and the pursuit of the passion.

key_insights.insight: Find a unique niche and lean into it; avoid being a clone of other artists.
key_insights.quote: Find like a really unique thing and just and just lean into that lean into that so hard like figure out even if it feels weird just make it make sense you know make it make it work for you because otherwise you're just going to be one in one of a hundred like clones

key_insights.insight: Sometimes, being different or 'weird' indicates untapped potential.
key_insights.quote: If it feels weird is that sometimes also an indication that there's a big opportunity there because it's it's yet to break or it's yet to be discovered

key_insights.insight: To predict if a record will be a success is impossible, but the core focus to create that 'big' record involves painfully concise effort.
key_insights.quote: If you think a record is the big record you just it's so painstakingly like like the effort is so concise like how to make this the best record ever

key_insights.insight: It's crucial to not let the high of success cause extreme lows.
key_insights.quote: You have to kind of suppress the high to avoid the low

key_insights.insight: It's important to have a fallback plan and to know you can start over if needed, which brings comfort and freedom to create.
key_insights.quote: If you have that feeling inside you that you can lose everything and be okay with it, that's maybe that's the key to success.

key_insights.insight: Success as a producer often requires diplomacy and relationship-building to bring different artists together.
key_insights.quote: Sometimes it's literally like being an ambassador,like talking to all these people and try to put a record together too.

actionable_advice.advice: If you want to be a DJ or artist, carve your own path by discovering and showcasing unique music.
actionable_advice.how_to: Dig deep into local scenes, explore different cultures, and bring these unheard sounds to new audiences.

actionable_advice.advice: To ensure a record has its best chance of surviving, work as an ambassador and be diplomatic to people involved.
actionable_advice.how_to: Talk to all the people that need to be involved and try to put the record together by finding who can play guitar, vocals, or anything it needs.

actionable_advice.advice: If you are caught up by management or someone else, make sure they let you be yourself and believe in you.
actionable_advice.how_to: Be vocal on how you wish to be seen, and have them follow you instead of them setting the narrative.

actionable_advice.advice: For anyone in a high-performance career, balance work with time to recharge and focus on mental well-being to avoid burn out.
actionable_advice.how_to: Take a break even if it is not needed, say no and take time for yourself, family, and children.

actionable_advice.advice: To understand what a day-to-day job in music is like, take your children on the road with you.
actionable_advice.how_to: Show them the gym, the press, what takes to be ready for a show, and what it's like to be in the studio.

memorable_stories: Being expelled from military school and readmitted because it cost the family a lot of money.
Shop lifting samplers from guitar center in highschool.
Traveling to India and finding rare Beatles records, plus buying Indian soundtracks to sell in London.
Working as a social worker and getting beaten down by the bureaucracy.
Traveling to Brazil to discover funk music and working with M.I.A.
contrarian_claims: Technical skills are secondary in achieving success as a DJ/producer.
That the most creative people have bipolar tendencies.
That you have to suppress the high to avoid the low, like an athlete.
Dating someone else that is in the music business is bad for a relationship.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Diplo: College Dropout To World's Most Iconic DJ
